question_text: An LED light bulb has an efficiency of 85%, while an old incandescent bulb has an efficiency of 5%. If both use 60 J of electrical energy, calculate the difference in useful light energy produced by each bulb.
explanation: LED produces 85% of 60 J = 51 J of light. Incandescent produces 5% of 60 J = 3 J of light. The difference is 51 - 3 = 48 J. This shows why LEDs are much more efficient at converting electrical energy into useful light.
